description:
You are the configuration manager for a small micro-service project that stores
    its settings in YAML and TOML files located under /home/user/configs/.
    The developers have asked you to refactor the existing configuration, add a
    new consolidated YAML file, and create a machine-readable change-tracking log.

    STEP-BY-STEP OBJECTIVES
    1. Preparation  
       1.1. Navigate to /home/user/configs/.  
       1.2. Familiarise yourself with the two pre-existing files:  
            • /home/user/configs/app.yml  
            • /home/user/configs/db.toml  

    2. Edit app.yml  
       2.1. Keep all indentation at exactly two spaces per level.  
       2.2. Re-order the top-level keys so they are strictly alphabetical
            (ASCII sort: a–z, all lowercase).  
       2.3. Make the following value changes or additions:  
            • debug: change from true → false  
            • version: update from 2.0.3 → 2.1.0  
            • database.host: change from localhost → db.internal.local  
            • Add a brand-new list key “features” (alphabetically placed) with the
              values [login, upload, analytics] on one YAML line.  

    3. Edit db.toml  
       3.1. In the [connection] table, change:  
            • host = "localhost"     → "db.internal.local"  
            • port = 5432            → 5434  
       3.2. In the [settings] table, add a new line  
            cache_max_size = 256  
            (keep existing max_connections line intact).  
       3.3. Preserve comments (if any) and the original ordering of keys inside each
            TOML table except for the newly inserted line, which must be placed at the
            end of the [settings] table.

    4. Create a consolidated YAML  
       4.1. File path: /home/user/configs/combined.yml  
       4.2. Expected structure (exact key names & ordering):  
            application:  
              app_name: SampleApp  
              version: 2.1.0  
            database:  
              host: db.internal.local  
              port: 5434  

    5. Produce a change-tracking log  
       5.1. File path: /home/user/configs/changes.log  
       5.2. Append (or create) exactly ONE log line per individual scalar value you
            modified in steps 2–3.  
       5.3. Log line format (strictly one line, no trailing spaces):  
            2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | <FILE_PATH> | <KEY_PATH> | <OLD_VALUE> -> <NEW_VALUE>  
            • FILE_PATH  – absolute path of the file you edited.  
            • KEY_PATH   – dot-notation for YAML or table.key notation for TOML.  
            • OLD_VALUE  – the value before you touched the file; write “(None)”
              if the key did not exist.  
            • NEW_VALUE  – the final value you wrote.  
       5.4. For this particular task you will have SIX log lines in total, all using
            the literal timestamp “2023-01-01T00:00:00Z”.

    6. Verification checklist before you finish  
       • /home/user/configs/app.yml top-level keys are in alphabetical order.  
       • /home/user/configs/db.toml contains the new cache_max_size line.  
       • /home/user/configs/combined.yml exactly matches the structure in step 4.  
       • /home/user/configs/changes.log has exactly six lines, each following the
         precise format in step 5.2 and sorted in any order you like.  

    NOTE
    • You do NOT need root privileges.  
    • Use any text editor or command-line utilities you prefer.  
    • Do not alter any other files outside /home/user/configs/.  
    • The automated test harness will parse the YAML, TOML and log files to confirm
      that every requirement above has been met.
truth:
Initial file contents before the agent starts:

    1. /home/user/configs/app.yml
    --------------------------------
    app_name: SampleApp
    debug: true
    version: 2.0.3
    database:
      host: localhost
      port: 5432
    --------------------------------

    2. /home/user/configs/db.toml
    --------------------------------
    [connection]
    host = "localhost"
    port = 5432
    user = "appuser"

    [settings]
    max_connections = 150
    --------------------------------

    Expected final file contents after successful completion:

    1. /home/user/configs/app.yml
    --------------------------------
    app_name: SampleApp
    database:
      host: db.internal.local
      port: 5432
    debug: false
    features: [login, upload, analytics]
    version: 2.1.0
    --------------------------------
    (exact two-space indentation and alphabetical ordering of top-level keys)

    2. /home/user/configs/db.toml
    --------------------------------
    [connection]
    host = "db.internal.local"
    port = 5434
    user = "appuser"

    [settings]
    max_connections = 150
    cache_max_size = 256
    --------------------------------

    3. /home/user/configs/combined.yml
    --------------------------------
    application:
      app_name: SampleApp
      version: 2.1.0
    database:
      host: db.internal.local
      port: 5434
    --------------------------------

    4. /home/user/configs/changes.log
    --------------------------------
    2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | /home/user/configs/app.yml | debug | true -> false
    2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | /home/user/configs/app.yml | version | 2.0.3 -> 2.1.0
    2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | /home/user/configs/app.yml | database.host | localhost -> db.internal.local
    2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | /home/user/configs/db.toml | connection.host | localhost -> db.internal.local
    2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | /home/user/configs/db.toml | connection.port | 5432 -> 5434
    2023-01-01T00:00:00Z | /home/user/configs/db.toml | settings.cache_max_size | (None) -> 256
    --------------------------------
